prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene (C₆H₅NO₂) was computed using DFT with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (density-fitted, PySCF backend) in the gas phase. The geometry was not re-optimized; the Fukui indices (f⁺ for electrophilicity, f⁻ for nucleophilicity, and f⁰) were calculated as single-point properties on the user-supplied input structure via the finite-difference method using N+1 (cation) and N−1 (anion) states (both charge 0 multiplicity 1 → charge ±1 multiplicity 2, Mulliken charges). Both perturbed-state SCFs converged, and charge conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000). The most electrophilic atom is the carbon ortho to the NO₂ group (atom 4, f⁺ = 0.4964), and the most nucleophilic is the meta carbon (atom 5, f⁻ = 0.1073). All 14 atoms' Fukui indices, dual descriptors, and interpretation are reported in the table above. A reactivity map plot is available at the cited path. Integrity check status: trustworthy = true.
